name: The Fully Loaded Iceberg Wedge Salad
description: Ice-cold iceberg wedges under a thick, chunky blue cheese dressing, piled with crispy bacon, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and chives. The crisp, cold, tangy foil that cuts right through rich BBQ ribs. From Serious Eats (your 4-star pick). Serves ~6-8.
recipeIngredient: 2 heads iceberg lettuce, cut into wedges and chilled
4 ounces blue cheese, crumbled (plus more for topping)
1 tablespoon white wine vinegar (or lemon juice)
6 slices bacon, cooked crisp and crumbled
1 cup cherry or grape tomatoes, halved
Fresh chives, thinly sliced, for garnish
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions: Make the dressing: whisk (or lightly blend) the blue cheese, mayonnaise, buttermilk, sour cream, and vinegar with salt and pepper - leave plenty of cheese in chunks for texture. Chill.
Cut the iceberg into wedges and keep them ice-cold and crisp until serving.
Set a wedge on each plate. Spoon the blue cheese dressing generously over the top.
Pile on the crumbled bacon, tomatoes, red onion, extra blue cheese crumbles, and a shower of chives. Finish with cracked black pepper. Serve cold.
author: Serious Eats

ai.cliffNotes: Cold, crisp iceberg wedges under chunky blue cheese dressing with bacon, tomato, red onion, and chives. The refreshing counterpoint to rich ribs.
keyTips: Keep the iceberg ice-cold and bone-dry - the whole appeal is the shattering-cold crunch against the warm ribs, so chill the wedges and dress them at the last second.
Leave the blue cheese chunky in the dressing rather than blending it smooth; you want pockets of funk, not a uniform sauce.
Cook the bacon extra crisp and crumble it - limp bacon disappears against the cold lettuce.
Dress to order right before serving; a dressed wedge wilts fast.
